name: Ramesh Vishwakarma
bio: Master weaver Ramesh Vishwakarma continues a 200-year family tradition of Banarasi silk weaving in the narrow lanes of Varanasi. His workshop produces some of the finest zari work in India, blending Mughal-era motifs with contemporary design sensibilities. Ramesh learned the craft at age 8 from his grandfather and has since trained over 30 apprentices.

name: Lakshmi Sundaram
bio: Lakshmi Sundaram is one of the few women master weavers in Kanchipuram. She specializes in temple border designs and has pioneered new color combinations that appeal to modern brides while honoring traditional motifs. Her family's workshop uses only pure mulberry silk and real gold-dipped zari.

name: Mohammad Dar
bio: Ustad Mohammad Dar is a living legend of Kashmiri Sozni embroidery. His Pashmina shawls are collector's items, each taking 6 to 18 months to complete. Working from his atelier in Srinagar's old city, he maintains the ancient Kani weaving technique alongside Sozni needlework that can feature over 50 different colors in a single piece.
